jokes.text: Then at night, I've got to drive around Newark looking for my runaway daughter.
jokes.setup: Casual conversation about vacation plans
jokes.punchline: Drops casual mention of personal family crisis
jokes.explanation: Perfect example of 30 Rock's casual darkness - takes a dire situation and presents it as mundane workplace complaint. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: With your flat, manly, milking thumbs and your long, graceful knuckle hair
jokes.setup: Kenneth complimenting Liz's hands
jokes.punchline: Describes hands in hilariously unflattering yet weirdly poetic terms
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Kenneth - his weird rural perspective creating unexpectedly specific and bizarre imagery. 'Knuckle hair' is inspired absurdity.

jokes.text: after the French custom, people wear dark socks to the beach
jokes.setup: Liz describing her vacation destination amenities
jokes.punchline: Mentions bizarre fake French custom as if it's a selling point
jokes.explanation: Absurdist detail that reveals Liz's willingness to accept any vacation as exotic. The specificity of 'dark socks' makes it memorable.

jokes.text: being worn by Dame Judi Dench's mother
jokes.setup: Liz mentions seeing her swimsuit in Us Weekly worn by Dame Judi Dench
jokes.punchline: Adds 'mother' - implying it's for extremely elderly women
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation joke - starts with respected actress, then reveals it's actually for someone even older. Great economy with just two words.

jokes.text: Oh, no! I must have ox fever! When did I walk barefoot near an ox?
jokes.setup: Kenneth claiming he has the constitution of an ox
jokes.punchline: Immediately gets sick and assumes it's ox-related
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth logic - takes metaphor literally and creates bizarre medical condition. Character integration is flawless.

jokes.text: Oh, I forgot to hit send. Just hold on a second. There you go.
jokes.setup: Liz devastated about cancelled vacation, Cerie claims she texted
jokes.punchline: Reveals she never sent the text and does it right then
jokes.explanation: Perfect millennial character beat - casual cruelty through technological incompetence. The timing makes it especially painful.

jokes.text: I once had a very under-cooked fish sandwich at a parade.
jokes.setup: Jack asking if Elisa has had sushi
jokes.punchline: Describes weirdly specific non-sushi experience
jokes.explanation: Establishes class difference through food experience. The parade detail adds absurd specificity that makes it memorable.

jokes.text: Oh, that reminds me. I owe Lou Dobbs a call.
jokes.setup: Elisa describing patient disconnected from reality
jokes.punchline: Jack casually equates Lou Dobbs with dementia patient
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack line - casually insulting public figure while revealing his social circle. Economy is excellent - one line, multiple layers.

jokes.text: Especially those whose grandmothers are addicted to online poker.
jokes.setup: Explaining why she works two jobs like regular people
jokes.punchline: Adds specific personal detail about gambling grandmother
jokes.explanation: Adds character depth through unexpected family detail. The specificity of 'online poker' modernizes the gambling trope.

jokes.text: Wow. That does not sound right.
jokes.setup: Jack struggling with how to refer to Elisa's ethnicity
jokes.punchline: Even saying 'Puerto Rican' sounds wrong to his privileged ears
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - reveals Jack's privileged discomfort with basic ethnic terminology. Perfect cringe moment.

jokes.text: I'm 'A-oh-boy.'
jokes.setup: Asked about being sick
jokes.punchline: Kenneth's folksy way of saying 'okay' sounds like a disease
jokes.explanation: Classic Kenneth - his rural expressions sound like medical conditions. Good character-specific wordplay.

jokes.text: Sully! Brett Favre, right?
jokes.setup: Liz encountering blue-collar crew member named Sully
jokes.punchline: Assumes all guys named Sully must love Brett Favre
jokes.explanation: Stereotypical but works for character - Liz making assumptions about blue-collar workers. Functional rather than inspired.

jokes.text: My favorite part is attending executions.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman saying flu shots aren't his favorite part of medicine
jokes.punchline: Reveals his favorite part is attending executions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman - casually mentions something deeply disturbing as if it's normal medical practice. Dark and memorable.

jokes.text: A more refined class of prostitute... For me, it's really about the companionship.
jokes.setup: Jack listing perks of being elite
jokes.punchline: Dr. Spaceman adds prostitutes then clarifies it's about companionship
jokes.explanation: Classic Spaceman - inappropriate overshare followed by attempt to make it sound classy. Perfect character beat.

jokes.text: My grandfather dug out the White Haven quarry, and my other grandfather filled it back in with the sludge from the eraser factory.
jokes.setup: Liz asserting her working-class roots
jokes.punchline: Describes family history that perfectly cancels itself out
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic - two grandfathers whose work completely cancels out. Perfect metaphor for futile working-class struggle.

jokes.text: When is modern science going to find a cure for a woman's mouth?
jokes.setup: Liz arguing about fairness
jokes.punchline: Jack's misogynistic comment about women talking
jokes.explanation: Typical Jack misogyny presented as pseudo-scientific inquiry. Character-appropriate but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Hey, it's me. Matlock. I need your help.
jokes.setup: Dementia patient speaks
jokes.punchline: Thinks he's TV detective Matlock
jokes.explanation: Simple dementia joke using iconic TV character. Functional but predictable for the setup.

jokes.text: Is that a beak? - Yes! - No. Yes!
jokes.setup: Jack examining patient's foot
jokes.punchline: Foot deformity so bad it might be a beak
jokes.explanation: Visual gag about grotesque medical condition. The contradictory responses add to the absurdity.

jokes.text: Then why am I telling you you look like Tootsie today?
jokes.setup: Tracy claims flu shot is truth serum, Liz denies it
jokes.punchline: Tracy proves it by insulting Liz's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline structure with Tracy's particular brand of logic. The Tootsie reference is specific and mean.

jokes.text: Wrapped in a Confederate Flag, fried and fed to dogs.
jokes.setup: Kenneth mentions traditional burial of a Parcell man
jokes.punchline: Describes horrific burial ritual matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Peak Kenneth absurdity - combines Southern heritage with grotesque burial practice. Dark, specific, and perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I saw the trailer when I went to see Alvin and the Chipmunks.
jokes.setup: Jack assuming Liz saw Michael Moore movie
jokes.punchline: Liz reveals she only saw trailer at kids' movie
jokes.explanation: Reveals Liz's lowbrow movie tastes while deflating Jack's assumptions about her political engagement.

jokes.text: This makes me want to 'shoop'! 'Shoop, shoop, shoop, shoop, shoop!'
jokes.setup: Crew getting excited about fighting the power
jokes.punchline: References Salt-N-Pepa song in bizarre context
jokes.explanation: Random 90s music reference that doesn't quite land. The enthusiasm is funny but the connection is weak.

jokes.text: It was a bad one. Jenna had to put my tongue guard in.
jokes.setup: Tracy mentions having a brainstorm
jokes.punchline: Reveals he had literal seizure requiring medical intervention
jokes.explanation: Good Tracy absurdity - takes common expression literally with medical consequences. The image of Jenna helping is perfect.

jokes.text: My what? ...No. ...With what? My arms? ...That would be the worst part!
jokes.setup: Kenneth suggesting they could get soup themselves
jokes.punchline: Tracy and Jenna horrified at each step of normal human activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalating absurdity showing how disconnected from reality celebrities are. Each response gets more ridiculous.

jokes.text: Without the crew, we'd just be two amazing people succeeding in a vacuum.
jokes.setup: Jenna trying to motivate Tracy to help crew
jokes.punchline: Completely self-absorbed reasoning for helping others
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jenna moment - even her attempts at altruism are narcissistic. Good character consistency.

jokes.text: Because it's their birthday.
jokes.setup: Trying to motivate Tracy to help the crew
jokes.punchline: Jenna's bizarre logic that they should help because it's the crew's birthday
jokes.explanation: Jenna's self-centered logic creates nonsensical motivation. Short and sweet character beat.

jokes.text: Thank you, Roger. This table was super-duper.
jokes.setup: Elisa fantasizing about fancy restaurant experience
jokes.punchline: Uses childish language for sophisticated situation
jokes.explanation: Shows Elisa's class background through linguistic mismatch. 'Super-duper' in fancy restaurant is endearing.

jokes.text: But I spend all my time with the BigVegetable.
jokes.setup: Elisa saying she lives in the Big Apple
jokes.punchline: Calls her dementia patient 'the BigVegetable'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay contrasting NYC nickname with medical term for unresponsive patient. Dark but clever.

jokes.text: Musical sequence about taking Mr. Templeton out
jokes.setup: Jack trying to convince Elisa to go out
jokes.punchline: Breaks into elaborate musical number about elderly patient
jokes.explanation: Absurdist musical break about elder abuse is peak 30 Rock weirdness. High concept but slightly indulgent.

jokes.text: I am so happy, the number four, the letter 'U'.
jokes.setup: Liz expressing happiness about vacation being back on
jokes.punchline: Says '4U' in the most awkward way possible
jokes.explanation: Perfect Liz awkwardness - trying to be casual with text speak but making it weird. Character-specific humor.

jokes.text: Stay away, sick ones!
jokes.setup: Liz realizing everyone around her is sick
jokes.punchline: Dramatic declaration while presumably fleeing
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment with Liz's germaphobia. The medieval language adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: If you want a shot... you're going to have to dance for it.
jokes.setup: Liz begging for flu shot after moral stance
jokes.punchline: Jack makes her literally dance for medical care
jokes.explanation: Perfect power dynamic reversal. Jack's cruelty and Liz's desperation create painful comedy.

jokes.text: I'm cheating on my wife.
jokes.setup: Discussing doctor-patient confidentiality
jokes.punchline: Dr. Spaceman immediately overshares personal information
jokes.explanation: Perfect Spaceman - completely misunderstands professional ethics and overshares. Excellent timing and character work.

jokes.text: Yeah, I get it. You went shopping. I don't need the montage.
jokes.setup: Tracy and Jenna starting to explain their shopping trip
jokes.punchline: Liz breaks fourth wall by referencing TV montage trope
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about TV conventions. Liz's exhaustion with celebrity antics translates to audience fatigue with clichés.

jokes.text: I also want to thank you for controlling yourself sexually while we spend time together on this adventure.
jokes.setup: Tracy talking to Jenna after shopping
jokes.punchline: Assumes any time with woman requires sexual restraint
jokes.explanation: Tracy's misogynistic assumptions presented as praise. Character-appropriate but predictable.

jokes.text: Oh! My purse!
jokes.setup: Presumably Mr. Templeton grabbing or doing something to purse
jokes.punchline: Elisa's shocked reaction to patient's unexpected behavior
jokes.explanation: Simple visual gag. Context suggests patient unexpectedly grabbed purse. Functional but unremarkable.

jokes.text: About 24 hours after receiving the flu shot... you develop a distinctive rash... at the injection site.
jokes.setup: Spaceman giving medical information
jokes.punchline: Revealing Liz will be caught because of rash
jokes.explanation: Good plot-driving comedy moment. Sets up Liz's exposure with medical detail that feels authentic to Spaceman.

jokes.text: I wasn't resting! A Parcell man never lies down on the job. Unless that job involves... milking pig teats.
jokes.setup: Kenneth defending his work ethic
jokes.punchline: Very specific and gross exception to the rule
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth moment - noble sentiment undercut by grotesque rural detail. The specificity of 'pig teats' is inspired.

jokes.text: Oh... Oh, ma chère maman! Pourquoi? Pourquoi m' as tu laissè?
jokes.setup: Liz claiming Kenneth is having a fever dream and they're speaking French
jokes.punchline: Kenneth actually responds in French, crying about his mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Kenneth moment - reveals unexpected French fluency through emotional outburst. Adds mysterious depth to character.

jokes.text: But he's going to need a helmet.
jokes.setup: Jack planning to take Mr. Templeton out
jokes.punchline: Casually mentions dementia patient needs protective headgear
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about elder care safety. The casual mention makes the absurd situation more unsettling.

jokes.text: Oh, the customers are so sad. 'Can I help you?' 'Yes. What time do you start throwing out doughnuts?'
jokes.setup: Elisa describing late shift at Dunkin' Donuts
jokes.punchline: Depicts desperate late-night customers asking for discarded food
jokes.explanation: Poignant and funny observation about late-night urban life. Shows class awareness and empathy for working people.

jokes.text: A man comes at night. He comes to the house and takes me. He wears a suit. His hair is thick... like a Bolshevik Commissar.
jokes.setup: Dementia patient describing Jack's visits
jokes.punchline: Describes Jack in terms of Communist revolutionary
jokes.explanation: Brilliant description of Jack through confused elderly perspective. 'Bolshevik Commissar' is unexpectedly specific and perfect.

jokes.text: He made me watch a giraffe with the legs of a man! He gave money to a silver man! He ate a plate of fire!
jokes.setup: Patient describing their night out
jokes.punchline: Describes Lion King, tipping, and fancy food through dementia filter
jokes.explanation: Incredible absurdist poetry - reframes fancy NYC evening through confused elderly lens. Each image is perfectly distorted and memorable.

jokes.text: But now, he owns a railroad!
jokes.setup: Continuing confused description
jokes.punchline: Somehow concludes Jack owns a railroad
jokes.explanation: Continues the patient's confused narrative with another absurd detail. Less striking than previous lines but maintains the surreal logic.

jokes.text: And what's the best medicine? Medicine? Laughter.
jokes.setup: Jenna asking rhetorical question about best medicine
jokes.punchline: Tracy gives literal answer before Jenna corrects with cliché
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline with Tracy's literal thinking disrupting Jenna's attempt at wisdom. Shows their different types of stupidity.

jokes.text: And then... I want to go to a Negro bar.
jokes.setup: Patient making deals with Jack
jokes.punchline: Uses outdated racial terminology casually
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate for elderly man but relies on outdated language for shock value. Somewhat lazy social commentary.

jokes.text: What? Why aren't you laughing? This is happening to Liz!
jokes.setup: Tracy and Jenna's physical comedy not getting laughs
jokes.punchline: They think crew should laugh because Liz is the target
jokes.explanation: Good meta-comedy about comedy itself. Tracy and Jenna's confusion about audience loyalty is character-perfect.

jokes.text: She got a flu shot! She... She lied to us! Give us back our meat plate!
jokes.setup: Crew discovering Liz got flu shot
jokes.punchline: Demands return of gift they gave her
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier gift. The specificity of 'meat plate' and demanding it back shows working-class vindictiveness.

jokes.text: This one has beach socks and ice cream and sandwich turtles!
jokes.setup: Liz trying to justify her vacation
jokes.punchline: Lists increasingly absurd vacation amenities
jokes.explanation: 'Sandwich turtles' is inspired absurdity. Liz's desperation makes her invent increasingly ridiculous amenities.

jokes.text: A Filipino gentleman. His name is Banyani. I want to take him as my island lover!
jokes.setup: Liz admitting there's a gentleman involved
jokes.punchline: Reveals creepy vacation sexual fantasy
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe moment - Liz's sexual desperation creates uncomfortable colonial fantasy. 'Island lover' is perfectly creepy.

jokes.text: It kind of peters out after that. I just suck pie off my sweatshirt for the next half hour.
jokes.setup: Liz describing her confession aftermath
jokes.punchline: Admits to pathetic food-related behavior
jokes.explanation: Peak Liz pathetic behavior. The visual of sucking pie off sweatshirt is perfectly degrading and specific.

jokes.text: Both ends! Both ends!
jokes.setup: Jack and Liz both getting flu symptoms
jokes.punchline: Jack announces he's experiencing flu symptoms from both ends
jokes.explanation: Basic bodily function humor. Gets the job done but relies on gross-out rather than wit.

jokes.text: Final musical sequence about Mr. Templeton
jokes.setup: Return to musical theme from earlier
jokes.punchline: Elaborate song about taking dementia patient out continues
jokes.explanation: Callback to earlier musical bit but less impactful. The absurdity remains but feels more indulgent than funny.
